description_long: This is a rounded, earthenware vase with a narrow neck and two small loop handles on either side. The surface is decorated with bold, reddish-brown and white painted motifs, including a stylized anthropomorphic figure with outstretched arms and a circular body on the front. The back features a large, zigzag or 'W'-shaped pattern in similar colors. The neck of the vase is slightly flared and shows two raised circular elements, possibly representing eyes or ornamental knobs. The surface appears matte and slightly rough, with visible textural variations typical of hand-formed pottery. The overall form and decoration suggest a traditional or indigenous artistic influence, making it a striking decorative piece.
description: A rounded, hand-crafted earthenware vase with painted anthropomorphic and zigzag motifs in reddish-brown and white, featuring a narrow neck and two small side handles.
description_view_0: Front view shows the anthropomorphic figure with outstretched arms and circular body, as well as the two raised knobs on the neck.
description_view_1: Side view reveals one of the loop handles and the transition from the figure motif to the zigzag pattern.
description_view_2: Back view highlights the large zigzag or 'W'-shaped motif, with both handles visible and no anthropomorphic figure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the other handle and the edge of both the zigzag and anthropomorphic motifs.
